intro.title: Classes and objects
intro.bodyMarkdown:
A class is a blueprint; an object is an instance. Define with `class Dog:` and instantiate with `Dog()`.

Objects bundle data (attributes) and behavior (methods). `self` refers to the current instance in methods.

Classes organize code around real-world nouns — users, accounts, games.

challenges.title: Easy: define class
challenges.starterCode:
class Cat:
    pass

challenges.title: Easy: create instance
Medium: __init__
Medium: self attribute
Hard: method call
Hard: two instances
challenges.starterCode:
class P:
 def __init__(self,n): self.n=n
a=P(1); b=P(2)
ok=a.n!=b.n

challenges.title: Expert: repr basics
challenges.starterCode:
class Point:
 def __init__(self,x,y): self.x,self.y=x,y
 def __repr__(self): return f"Point({self.x}, {self.y})"
r=repr(Point(1,2))

challenges.title: Expert: class attribute
challenges.starterCode:
class Dog:
 species="Canis"
d1=Dog(); d2=Dog()
ok=d1.species==d2.species==Dog.species

challenges.title: Expert: docstring class

intro.title: Instance methods
intro.bodyMarkdown:
Methods are functions on the class taking `self` first. Call via `obj.method()`.

They read and modify instance state. Keep methods focused on one responsibility.

Avoid putting unrelated logic in one giant method — split helpers.

challenges.title: Easy: define method
challenges.starterCode:
class A:
    def go(self): pass

challenges.title: Medium: modify self
challenges.starterCode:
class C:
 def __init__(self): self.n=0
 def add(self): self.n+=1
c=C(); c.add(); c.add()

challenges.title: Medium: return from method
Hard: chain calls
challenges.starterCode:
class C:
 def __init__(self): self.n=0
 def bump(self): self.n+=1; return self
c=C().bump().bump().bump(); n=c.n

challenges.title: Hard: private convention
Expert: classmethod intro
Expert: staticmethod intro
Expert: property later
Expert: dunder str

intro.title: Inheritance
intro.bodyMarkdown:
Subclass inherits parent: `class Cat(Animal):`. Override methods for specialized behavior.

Use `super()` to call parent implementation. Favor composition when inheritance is shallow.

Liskov: subclass should be substitutable for parent.

challenges.title: Easy: subclass syntax
challenges.starterCode:
class Child(Parent):
    pass

validation.setupCode:
class P:
 def m(self): return 1
class Child(P): pass
ok=Child().m()==1

challenges.title: Medium: override
Medium: super call
Hard: isinstance subclass
challenges.starterCode:
class P: pass
class C(P): pass
ok=isinstance(C(),P)

challenges.title: Hard: extend __init__
Expert: mro
challenges.starterCode:

class B(A): pass
class C(B): pass
mro=C.__mro__

challenges.title: Expert: composition over
Expert: abstract base
Expert: multiple inheritance

intro.title: Encapsulation
intro.bodyMarkdown:
Hide internal details; expose clean public API. Use `_name` convention for internal use.

Properties can guard attribute access. Do not expose mutable internals casually.

Encapsulation reduces coupling between components.

challenges.title: Easy: underscore attr
Easy: public method
challenges.starterCode:
class A:
    def show(self): return 1

challenges.title: Medium: no direct mutate
challenges.starterCode:
class A:
 def __init__(self): self._b=100
 def add(self,n): self._b+=n
a=A(); a.add(50); b=a._b

challenges.title: Medium: validate in method
Hard: property getter
Hard: read-only pattern
Expert: name mangling

Expert: leaky abstraction
challenges.starterCode:
class Account:
 def __init__(self): self._balance=100
 def deposit(self,n): self._balance+=n
 def balance(self): return self._balance
a=Account(); a.deposit(50); b=a.balance()

challenges.title: Expert: dataclass private

intro.title: Polymorphism
intro.bodyMarkdown:
Same interface, different behavior. Duck typing: if it quacks like a duck, use it.

Functions accepting any object with required methods work polymorphically.

Inheritance is one form; protocols and ABCs formalize expectations.

challenges.title: Easy: loop polymorphic
challenges.starterCode:
animals=[Cat(),Dog()]
for a in animals: print(a.speak())

challenges.title: Medium: function accepts any
Medium: duck typing
Hard: no isinstance needed
Hard: protocol style
Expert: ABC enforce
Expert: operator overloading
Expert: double dispatch
Expert: structural subtyping

questions.prompt: Duck typing: if it has method:

intro.title: Dunder methods
intro.bodyMarkdown:
Double-underscore methods customize behavior: `__str__`, `__repr__`, `__len__`, `__eq__`.

They integrate objects with Python syntax — print, len, ==, +.

Implement `__repr__` for developers; `__str__` for users.

challenges.title: Easy: __str__
challenges.starterCode:
class A:
    def __str__(self): return "A"

challenges.title: Easy: __repr__
challenges.starterCode:
class A:
    def __repr__(self): return "A()"

challenges.title: Medium: __len__
Medium: __eq__
challenges.starterCode:
class A:
 def __init__(self,v): self.v=v
 def __eq__(self,o): return self.v==o.v
ok=A(1)==A(1)

challenges.title: Hard: __add__
Hard: __bool__
Expert: __iter__
Expert: __getitem__
Expert: __hash__
Expert: __slots__
challenges.starterCode:
class A:
 __slots__=("x",)
 def __init__(self): self.x=1
ok="x" in A.__slots__

questions.prompt: Expert: mutable eq needs care with:

intro.title: Properties
intro.bodyMarkdown:
`@property` turns method into attribute access. `@name.setter` validates writes.

Prefer properties over getters/setters when behavior may grow.

Read-only: property without setter.

challenges.title: Easy: @property
Medium: setter
Medium: validate setter
Hard: computed property
Hard: read only
Expert: deleter
Expert: property over get_set
Expert: cached property
challenges.starterCode:
from functools import cached_property
class C:
 _calls=0
 @cached_property
 def value(self):
  type(self)._calls+=1
  return 42
c=C()
_=c.value
_=c.value
calls=C._calls

challenges.title: Expert: dataclass field

questions.prompt: Expert: property vs public attr when:

intro.title: Dataclasses
intro.bodyMarkdown:
`@dataclass` auto-generates `__init__`, `__repr__`, comparisons from typed fields.

Great for data containers — less boilerplate than manual classes.

Use `field(default_factory=list)` for mutable defaults.
intro.sandboxCode:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class User:
    name: str
    score: int = 0

u = User("Ana", 42)
print(u)

challenges.title: Easy: import dataclass
challenges.starterCode: from dataclasses import dataclass

intro.title: Advanced OOP patterns
intro.bodyMarkdown:
Composition, mixins, factory functions, and dependency injection structure larger apps.

Keep inheritance depth low; prefer small focused classes.

Design for testability — inject dependencies, avoid globals.

challenges.title: Easy: composition has-a
Medium: factory function
Medium: mixin pattern
Hard: single responsibility
Hard: interface segregation
Expert: dependency injection
Expert: service layer
Expert: repository pattern
Expert: test doubles

intro.title: OOP design principles
intro.bodyMarkdown:
SOLID guides maintainable OOP: Single responsibility, Open/closed, Liskov, Interface segregation, Dependency inversion.

Name classes after concepts; methods after verbs. Cohesive modules.

Refactor when classes know too much about each other.

challenges.title: Easy: NotImplementedError
Medium: open closed extend
Medium: small interface
Hard: dependency invert
Hard: cohesion
challenges.starterCode:
class ReportBuilder:
 def add_row(self,r): return r
 def build(self,rows): return "\
".join(rows)
methods=[m for m in dir(ReportBuilder) if not m.startswith("_")]
ok="add_row" in methods and "build" in methods

names=[OrderService.__name__, EmailNotifier.__name__]
